chatbotKnowledge.answer: Moein's research focuses on Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Social Media Mining. He works on social support detection, hate/hope speech detection, sentiment analysis, language identification, and FinTech NLP — particularly cryptocurrency discourse analysis. He uses deep learning, transformers, and psycholinguistic features in his work.

Moein Shahiki Tash is a PhD researcher in Artificial Intelligence (NLP) at Instituto Politécnico Nacional (IPN), Mexico City, working at the Gelbukh Lab. He's also a Lecturer at Universidad Panamericana, CDMX, where he teaches graduate-level NLP courses. He has 12 journal papers and 11 conference papers published.

Moein is completing his PhD in Computer Science (NLP) at IPN Mexico City (2022-2026) at the Gelbukh Lab. He holds an M.Sc. (with Distinction) and B.Sc. in Computer Science from the University of Sistan and Baluchestan, Iran.

Moein has 12 journal papers (8 published in Q1/Q2 journals, 4 under review) and 11 conference papers. His Q1 publications appear in Scientific Reports, Cognitive Computation, Heliyon, and PLOS ONE. He's also published at ACL workshops, IberLEF, ICON, and DravidianLangTech.

Moein currently teaches NLP at Universidad Panamericana (since July 2025) and researches at IPN's Gelbukh Lab. Previously, he was a Machine Learning Engineer at Lidoma (2019-2022) and a Python Developer at Hi-Web (2017-2018).
You can reach Moein at moein.tash@gmail.com. He's also active on LinkedIn, GitHub, and Google Scholar — always open to academic collaboration and research discussions.
Moein is fluent in English, native in Persian and Baluchi, and has intermediate Spanish.

Moein's tech stack: Python, TensorFlow, Keras, Hugging Face Transformers, Scikit-learn, Pandas, NumPy, Streamlit, LangChain, FAISS, and Azure ML. His NLP expertise covers hate/hope speech detection, sentiment analysis, NER, code-mixed text handling, and language identification.

Some notable projects: a URL-based RAG chatbot (Streamlit + LangChain + FAISS), a bilingual social support dataset (English & Spanish), a crypto emotion analysis system using SenticNet, and the IberLEF 2026 SSD Shared Task he organizes.

Moein holds the SECIHTI Scholarship from the Government of Mexico, graduated his M.Sc. with Distinction, is the Main Organizer of the SSD Shared Task at IberLEF 2026, a Program Committee Member at SemEval-2027, and has reviewed 10+ articles for international conferences.
Moein is based in Mexico City, Mexico, where he's pursuing his PhD at IPN and teaching at Universidad Panamericana.

Moein's NLP research covers social support detection, hate speech detection, hope speech detection, sentiment analysis, language identification, and FinTech NLP. He works on cross-lingual NLP across English, Spanish, Dravidian languages, and Persian.
Moein has been a Lecturer at Universidad Panamericana, CDMX since July 2025, where he teaches graduate-level courses in Natural Language Processing (NLP).

Moein's FinTech NLP work analyzes cryptocurrency discourse on X (Twitter) using SenticNet and psycholinguistic features, correlating emotional trends with market price movements. He's published this in Q1 journals like Scientific Reports and Cognitive Computation.

Moein has worked extensively on hate speech detection across multiple languages, including Spanish (HOMOMEX 2023), Tamil and Telugu (DravidianLangTech), and English. He uses BERT, LSTM, and CNN architectures for these classification tasks.

Moein researches hope speech detection — identifying supportive and encouraging content online. He published the paper 'Exploring Multidimensional Aspects of Hope Speech Computationally' in Scientific Reports (Q1) and contributed to multiple IberLEF shared tasks on hope detection.

Social support detection is one of Moein's main research areas. He created a bilingual dataset (English & Spanish), developed ML/DL approaches published in Heliyon and PLOS ONE (both Q1), and is the Main Organizer of the SSD Shared Task at IberLEF 2026.
Moein is a PhD researcher at the Alexander Gelbukh Lab, part of the NLP & AI Research Group at Centro de Investigación en Computación (CIC), Instituto Politécnico Nacional (IPN) in Mexico City.
Moein is always open to academic collaboration and research discussions in NLP, social media mining, and AI. You can reach him at moein.tash@gmail.com or connect via LinkedIn.

greetings: Hello! I'm Moein's AI assistant. Feel free to ask me anything about Moein Tash.
